title: Procurement and Project Management Office Associate
matched_skills: Vendor & partner management (partial match for procurement)
missing_skills: Completed commercial vocational training (industrial clerk, freight forwarder, or equivalent — Boru holds university education)
Direct procurement / purchasing / supply chain management experience
ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Microsoft Dynamics) — not mentioned in CV
Bid comparison and contract negotiation with suppliers
Inventory planning and goods receipt inspection
ATA Carnet preparation and international trade documentation
key_requirements: Execute end-to-end procurement processes: requirements planning, bid comparison, ordering, delivery tracking
Manage supplier selection, evaluation, contract negotiations, and confidentiality agreements
Organize international shipments including trade fair logistics and ATA Carnets
Gather project status from stakeholders and prepare project reports / track deadlines
Maintain item and supplier data in ERP systems; support inventory planning
cv_highlights: Fluent in German, English, Chinese, and Cantonese — meets language requirement
Vendor & partner management experience at HSBC and Intelligent Power Corporation
Project reporting and governance track record (HSBC USD 8M programme, Hapag-Lloyd KPI reporting)
Cross-functional stakeholder coordination across EMEA and APAC
Shipping industry familiarity from Hapag-Lloyd (customer experience context)
gaps: No vocational commercial training (IHK Kaufmann, Speditionskaufmann, etc.) — a stated requirement
No hands-on procurement or purchasing role; vendor management was strategic/coordination only
No ERP system usage documented in CV
No logistics operations experience (ATA Carnets, goods receipt, trade fair shipments)
Role is part-time 20–30h in Nuremberg — ~600 km from candidate's location in Lüneburg
No defense or security sector background
recommended_emphasis: Highlight vendor and partner management experience at HSBC and IPC as closest proxy to supplier management
Emphasize organizational skills, structured reporting, and deadline tracking from Hapag-Lloyd PMO role
Reference shipping/logistics context at Hapag-Lloyd to signal domain familiarity
Stress multilingual ability (German, English) as a direct requirement match
If applicable, surface any ERP or procurement tooling exposure not currently listed in CV
recommendation: LOW FIT — Not recommended for tailored application. The role requires commercial vocational training and direct procurement/purchasing/logistics operations experience that Boru's profile does not cover. Core gaps include: no ERP experience, no hands-on procurement, and no trade documentation background. Additional practical concerns: part-time only (20–30h), located in Nuremberg (~600 km from Lüneburg), and a defense startup context with no sector match. Match score 47 is below the 50-point threshold.
